CONTENT.Fictional characters: Isabel Fernandez, fictional character in the book Refugee

SUMMARY: Fernández (Spanish pronunciation: [feɾˈnandeθ] ) is a Spanish  patronymic surname meaning "son of Fernando" of Germanic origin. The Germanic name Ferdinand that it derives from (Gothic: Frið-nanð) means "brave traveler." The Portuguese version of this surname is Fernandes. The Arabized version is Ibn Faranda and it was used by the Mozarabs and Muwallads in Al-Andalus. Fernández was on the list of Officers and Sailors in the First Voyage of Columbus. The name is  popular in Spanish speaking countries and former colonies. The Anglicization of this surname is Fernandez.
